steps.title: Understand the passage structure
steps.content: The passage traces a sequential process: (1) a surveyor makes a grid, (2) the site is dug and artifacts are recorded and mapped. The blank introduces the final step. The correct transition must signal that this sentence completes the sequence — not add an example, introduce a contrast, or point backward in time.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice, then confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'For instance,': Signals that this sentence is an example of the previous one — but removing artifacts is a distinct next step, not an illustration. ✗
  • Choice B — 'On the contrary,': Signals contrast or opposition — there is no contradiction between mapping artifacts and then removing them. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Earlier,': Points backward in time — removing, cataloging, and analyzing artifacts happens after, not before, the dig. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Finally,': Marks the concluding step in a sequence — correctly signals that removing, cataloging, and analyzing artifacts is the last stage of the dig process. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
